stories.name: default-server-initialization
stories.title: Implement default blossom server initialization for new identities
stories.description: Add DEFAULT_BLOSSOM_SERVERS constant and initializeDefaults() method to BlossomServerService. Hook into NostlingService.createIdentity() to automatically configure https://cdn.satellite.earth (label: 'Satellite CDN', position: 0) for new identities only. Existing identities remain unaffected.

includes: DEFAULT_BLOSSOM_SERVERS constant in BlossomServerService.ts
BlossomServerService.initializeDefaults(identityPubkey) method
NostlingService.createIdentity() integration point
Database persistence to blossom_servers table
Idempotent initialization (safe to call multiple times)
Backward compatibility (existing identities unchanged)

scenarios.name: new-identity-has-default-server
scenarios.description: Create new identity and verify default blossom server is automatically configured
user_steps: Launch Nostling app via Playwright
Create new identity with label 'Test Identity'
Query blossom_servers table via IPC
Verify entry exists with url='https://cdn.satellite.earth', label='Satellite CDN', position=0
assertions: blossom_servers table contains exactly 1 row for new identity
URL is 'https://cdn.satellite.earth'
Label is 'Satellite CDN'
Position is 0

stories.notes: Foundation story - no UI changes, purely backend service layer. Follows existing DEFAULT_RELAYS pattern. E2E test at e2e/default-blossom-server.spec.ts verifies default server initialization via UI identity creation.

stories.name: blossom-server-settings-ui
stories.title: Implement BlossomServerSettings UI component with full CRUD operations
stories.description: Replace stubbed BlossomServerSettings component with full implementation: server list with health status indicators (green/yellow/red), add server form with HTTPS validation, remove server with confirmation dialog, empty state, error handling with toast notifications and rollback. No drag-and-drop reordering (out of scope). Health checks performed on mount only.

includes: Server list rendering with URL, label, position, health indicator
Health status indicators (green=200 OK within 3s, yellow=checking, red=failed/timeout)
Health checks on component mount only (no polling)
Add server form (URL input + optional label input + Add button)
HTTPS validation (reject http:// URLs with error message)
Remove server button with confirmation dialog
Confirmation dialog text: 'Remove blossom server [URL]?' with Remove/Cancel buttons
Empty state message: 'No blossom servers configured. Add one to enable media uploads.'
Optimistic UI updates with rollback on IPC failure
Error toasts for failed operations (add/remove)
State management (fetch on mount, real-time updates)
Integration with existing blossom-api.ts IPC bridge
excludes: Drag-and-drop reordering (out of scope for MVP)

test_fixtures.description: Mock blossom-api.ts IPC responses for component testing
test_fixtures.reason: Component tests need isolated testing without main process - permanent test infrastructure

scenarios.name: add-custom-blossom-server
scenarios.description: User adds custom HTTPS blossom server via settings UI
user_steps: Launch Nostling app, select existing identity
Navigate to Blossom Servers settings (component rendered in isolation for Story 02)
Enter URL 'https://example-blossom.com' in URL field
Enter label 'Custom Server' in label field
Click 'Add' button
Verify server appears in list
Verify health indicator shows (yellow then green/red)
Attempt to add 'http://insecure.com'
Verify error toast appears with HTTPS requirement message
Verify insecure server NOT added to list
assertions: Custom server visible in list after add
Health indicator transitions from yellow to final state
HTTP URL rejected with toast notification
Server list count increases by 1 for valid add only

scenarios.name: remove-blossom-server-with-confirmation
scenarios.description: User removes blossom server with confirmation dialog flow
user_steps: Launch Nostling app with identity having 2+ servers
Navigate to Blossom Servers settings
Click 'Remove' button on second server
Verify confirmation dialog appears with text 'Remove blossom server [URL]?'
Click 'Cancel' button
Verify server still in list
Click 'Remove' button again
Click 'Remove' in confirmation dialog
Verify server removed from list
Verify success toast appears
assertions: Confirmation dialog shows correct URL
Cancel preserves server in list
Remove deletes server from list
Toast notification confirms success

scenarios.name: empty-state-display
scenarios.description: Empty state message shown when no servers configured
user_steps: Launch Nostling app with identity having no servers
Navigate to Blossom Servers settings
Verify empty state message: 'No blossom servers configured. Add one to enable media uploads.'
Verify 'Add Server' form still accessible
Add a server via form
Verify empty state disappears
Verify server list shows with new server
assertions: Empty state message visible when server count = 0
Add form accessible in empty state
Empty state hidden when server count > 0
Server list renders correctly after first add

scenarios.name: health-check-on-mount
scenarios.description: Health checks performed once on component mount, no polling
user_steps: Configure identity with 2 servers: one reachable, one unreachable
Navigate to Blossom Servers settings
Observe health indicators start as yellow (checking)
Wait for health checks to complete (max 3s timeout)
Verify reachable server shows green (200 OK)
Verify unreachable server shows red (failed/timeout)
Wait 10 seconds with component mounted
Verify no additional health check requests sent (no polling)
assertions: Initial health status is yellow for all servers
Reachable server transitions to green within 3s
Unreachable server transitions to red after timeout
No network requests after initial check (verified via network logs)

stories.notes: UI component implemented with health indicators, add/remove with confirmation, HTTPS validation, empty state, optimistic UI with rollback. E2E test at e2e/blossom-server-settings.spec.ts (5 tests). Preload bridge added for blossom IPC.

stories.name: settings-integration-and-e2e-verification
stories.title: Integrate BlossomServerSettings into identity settings and verify dual-instance media flow
stories.description: Add 'Blossom Servers' section to identity settings panel, render BlossomServerSettings component when identity is selected, ensure Chakra UI theme consistency. Verify complete end-to-end flow with dual-instance test: Instance A creates identity with default server, uploads media attachment, Instance B receives message and views media from default server URL.

includes: Locate identity settings panel component
Add 'Blossom Servers' section title
Render <BlossomServerSettings identityPubkey={selectedIdentity.pubkey} />
Position below relay configuration (or logical settings area)
Chakra UI theme consistency verification
Settings visibility when identity selected
Dual-instance E2E test implementation
Media upload flow verification with default server
Cross-instance media rendering verification

scenarios.name: settings-accessible-in-identity-panel
scenarios.description: Verify blossom server settings accessible from identity settings panel with correct theme
user_steps: Launch Nostling app, select identity
Navigate to identity settings panel
Verify 'Blossom Servers' section appears
Verify section positioned below relay configuration (or in logical settings area)
Verify Chakra UI theme consistency (colors, spacing, typography)
Verify default server from Story 01 appears in list
Verify health indicator present
assertions: Blossom Servers section visible in identity settings
Section renders with correct Chakra UI theme
Default server visible in list
UI matches existing settings patterns

scenarios.name: dual-instance-media-upload-with-default-server
scenarios.description: End-to-end verification: Instance A creates identity with default server, uploads media, Instance B receives and views media
user_steps: Instance A: Create new identity 'Alice'
Instance A: Navigate to Blossom Servers settings, verify default server configured
Instance A: Add Instance B's identity as contact via QR exchange
Instance B: Accept contact request from Instance A
Instance A: Select conversation with Instance B
Instance A: Click attachment button (paperclip icon)
Instance A: Select test image file (test-fixtures/sample-image.png)
Instance A: Verify attachment preview appears in compose area
Instance A: Click 'Send' button
Instance A: Verify message shows 'uploading' status
Instance A: Wait for upload completion (status → 'sent')
Instance A: Verify uploaded message shows inline thumbnail
Instance B: Verify message with media attachment appears in conversation
Instance B: Verify media thumbnail displays (loaded from blossom server URL)
Instance B: Click thumbnail to expand media
Instance B: Verify full-size media displays correctly
assertions: Instance A: Default server auto-configured on identity creation
Instance A: Attachment uploads to default server (verify URL in message event)
Instance A: Message status transitions: queued → uploading → sent
Instance B: Message received with blossom URL in imeta tags
Instance B: Thumbnail renders from blossom server URL
Instance B: Full-size media accessible and renders correctly
Network logs confirm upload to https://cdn.satellite.earth (or test blossom-server)
No manual server configuration required for media sharing

stories.notes: Integration completed as part of Story 02 - BlossomServerSettings rendered below RelayTable in relay-config view. Dual-instance UI verification confirms section renders on both instances. Full media upload flow deferred to separate verification (requires active blossom server).

destination.artifact: Instance B media rendering (dual-instance test verification)

dependency_notes: Stories are designed as independent vertical slices. Story 01 (backend) and Story 02 (UI component) can be implemented in parallel - Story 02 uses existing BlossomServerService methods (addServer, removeServer, listServers, checkHealth) which are already implemented. Story 03 (integration) requires Stories 01 and 02 completed to verify the full flow. No temporary mocks needed - all dependencies are existing implemented services or permanent test fixtures.
mock_resolution_plan: No temporary mocks used. Story 02 uses permanent test fixtures (FIXTURE-02-001) for component testing, which provide mock IPC responses for isolated component tests. These are permanent testing infrastructure and do not require resolution. The dual-instance test in Story 03 uses real services in Docker environment (no mocks).
